description: Aligns long sequences (>200 bp) to a sequence database using BWA 0.7.4 bwasw.
author: Heng Li, Broad Institute
plan:
## BWA.bwasw GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
BWA.bwasw is a GenePattern module that Aligns long sequences (>200 bp) to a sequence database using BWA 0.7.4 bwasw.

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 15 parameters:
- Required: BWA.index, read.file, output.prefix
- Optional: mate.read.file, match.score, mismatch.penalty, gap.open.penalty, gap.extension.penalty, band.width, min.score.threshold, threshold.coefficient, z.best.heuristics, max.sa.interval.size, min.num.seeds, num.threads

### 3. Command Line
```
perl /opt/genepatt/BWA.bwasw/src/bwa_bwasw_wrapper.pl -bwa <BWA_0.7.4>/bwa -libdir <libdir> -w1 <BWA.index> -p <read.file> -m <mate.read.file> -a <match.score> -b <mismatch.penalty> -q <gap.open.penalty> -r <gap.extension.penalty> -w <band.width> -t2 <min.score.threshold> -c <threshold.coefficient> -z <z.best.heuristics> -s <max.sa.interval.size> -n <min.num.seeds> -t1 <num.threads> -o <output.prefix>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: genepattern/bwa_modules\:v0.2

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: BWA.index
parameters.description: A zip file or directory containing BWA index files.

parameters.name: read.file
parameters.description: Single-end or first paired-end reads file in FASTA or FASTQ format.  For paired-end data, this should be the forward ("*_1" or "left") input file.

parameters.name: mate.read.file
parameters.description: The reverse ("*_2" or "right") reads file for paired-end reads in FASTA or FASTQ format.

parameters.name: gap.open.penalty
parameters.description: Gap open penalty.  The gap open penalty is the score taken away for the initiation of the gap in sequence. To make the match more significant you can try to make the gap penalty larger.

parameters.name: gap.extension.penalty
parameters.description: Gap extension penalty.  The gap extension penalty is added to the standard gap penalty for each base or residue in the gap.  To reduce long gaps, increase the extension gap penalty.

parameters.name: band.width
parameters.description: band width in the banded alignment

parameters.name: min.score.threshold
parameters.description: Minimum score threshold divided by the match score

parameters.name: threshold.coefficient
parameters.description: Coefficient for threshold adjustment according to query length

parameters.name: z.best.heuristics
parameters.description: Z-best heuristics. Specifying a higher number increases accuracy at the cost of speed

parameters.name: max.sa.interval.size
parameters.description: Maximum SA interval size for initiating a seed. Specifying a higher number increases accuracy at the cost of speed.

parameters.name: min.num.seeds
parameters.description: The minimum number of seeds contained in the best alignment from the forward-forward alignment process that allows the algorithm to skip performing the reverse alignment. BWA.bwasw tends to be faster and more accurate if the alignment is supported by more seeds.

parameters.name: output.prefix
parameters.description: Prefix to use for the output file name
